file-selector.size-limit: File size should less than {{size}} MiB

audit-log.export-tooltip: Must select start time and end time within 30 days

actor.title: Actor
actor.description: Search by the audit log actor

method.title: Method
method.description: Search by the method of audit log

level.title: Level
level.description: Search by the level of audit log

condition.input-value-press-enter: Input value, press Enter to confirm

or.description: Any of the following are true...

and.description: All of the following are true...

condition.add-root-condition-placeholder: Click "Add condition" or "Add condition group" to add condition
condition.description-tips: A condition is a rule that can be configured via an expression. For example, the condition "Environment is prod" will match issues executed in the "prod" environment.

subscription.trial-for-days: You can start a free trial for {{days}} days - no credit card required.

instance-assignment.missing-license-attention: You need to assign license to the instance to enable this feature.

subscription.request-n-days-trial: Request {{days}} days trial (no credit card required)

instance-count.title: Instance count limit
instance-count.runoutof: You have run out of your {{total}} instance quota.

free.title: Community
free.desc: Team essentials to manage database development lifecycle.

team.title: Pro
team.desc: More policies to standardize and facilitate collaboration across teams.

enterprise.title: Enterprise
enterprise.desc: Extra security, compliance, and permission features. Dedicated support with SLA.

purchase.free-description: Designed for solo devs, side-projects, and experiments.
purchase.pro-description: Built for teams creating production-ready applications.
purchase.enterprise-description: Scale your business securely while staying compliant.
purchase.cancel-confirm: Are you sure you want to cancel your subscription?
purchase.pending-description: Please wait while we confirm your payment. This usually takes a few seconds.
purchase.cancel-pending: Your subscription has been canceled and will end on {{date}}.

success.title: Successfully updated license
success.description: Premium features in subscription plan unlocked.

failure.title: Failed to update license
failure.description: Please check if your license is valid.

subscription.description: You can upload your Bytebase license to unlock pro/enterprise features.
subscription.require-subscription: This feature requires {{requiredPlan}} subscription, please purchase Bytebase license to unlock it.

validation.overflow: The {{resource}} ID is too long. It should have less than 64 characters.
validation.pattern: The {{resource}} ID can have lowercase letters, digits, or hyphens. It must start with a lowercase letter and end with a letter or number.
validation.duplicated: The {{resource}} ID already exists. Please choose another one.

resource-id.description: The {{resource}} ID is a unique identifier. You cannot change it after created.

classification.guide-intro: Edit the classification JSON below. An example template is provided when no classification is configured.
classification.guide-levels: "levels" defines sensitivity tiers with consecutive numbers starting from 1 (e.g., 1 = Public, 2 = Internal, 3 = Confidential).
classification.guide-classification: "classification" maps each category by ID. Leaf categories must reference a level number; parent categories omit the level.
classification.guide-hierarchy: IDs use a dash-separated hierarchy (e.g., "1" is a parent, "1-1" and "1-2" are its children).

global-rules.description: Use global rule to apply masking in batch, e.g. all columns named as "email" will be masked with the specific semantic type and apply the masking algorithm.

template.description: You can apply built-in semantic types from the template. Built-in template cannot be changed.

semantic-types.label: Columns having the same semantic type will use the same masking algorithms.

full-mask.substitution-label: Substitution is the string used to replace the original value, the max length of the string is 16 bytes.

range-mask.label: Range mask will replace the original value from start index to end index (end not included) with the substitution. The index starts from 0.

md5-mask.salt-label: Salt is used to generate a hash from the original value.

inner-outer-mask.inner-label: Inner mask masks the interior part of a string. The prefix and suffix represent the unmasked parts of the data.
inner-outer-mask.outer-label: Outer mask masks the left and right ends of a string. The prefix and suffix represent the masked parts of the data.

error.slice-invalid-number: Slice start or end is not a valid number
error.substitution-length: Substitution should be less than 16 bytes

form.auth-url-description: The link to OAuth login page
form.token-url-description: The API address for obtaining accessToken
form.user-info-url-description: The API address for obtaining user information by accessToken
form.scopes-description: A space-separated list of scopes to be carried when accessing the Auth URL
form.in-header-description: Send credentials using HTTP Basic Authorization
form.skip-tls-warning: Only enable this for development or testing purposes
form.issuer-url-description: The OIDC issuer URL (well-known configuration will be auto-discovered)
form.openid-scopes-description: Space-separated list of OIDC scopes (openid is required)
form.bind-dn-description: The DN of the user to bind as for search requests
form.base-dn-description: The base DN to search for users
form.user-filter-description: The filter to search for users (%s will be replaced with username)
form.identifier-tips: It should be a unique string. And if it is not in email format, it will be spliced with domain.
form.groups-description: Used for group-based access control (OIDC providers only)
form.provider-type-readonly-hint: Provider type cannot be changed after creation
form.name: Name
form.name-description: The display name to be shown to the users
form.domain-optional-hint: Optional: Use to concat with identifier if it is not in email format
form.configuration-description: Configure the connection settings for your identity provider
form.user-information-mapping-description: Maps the field names from user info API to the Bytebase user.
form.identity-provider-needed-information: Create your SSO application with the following information.
form.redirect-url-description: It will be used to fill in the appropriate field in the identity provider application form.
form.oauth2-description: Connect with OAuth 2.0 providers like Google, GitHub, GitLab
form.oidc-description: Connect with OpenID Connect providers like Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD), Okta, Auth0
form.ldap-description: Connect with LDAP providers like Microsoft Active Directory, OpenLDAP
form.microsoft-entra-template-description: Sign in with Microsoft Entra ID
form.type-description: Choose the type of identity provider you want to configure
form.template-description: Choose a pre-configured template or set up a custom OAuth 2.0 provider
form.general-setting-description: Provide basic information about your identity provider.

sso.description: Single Sign-On (SSO) is an authentication method that enables users to authenticate with multiple applications and websites via a single set of credentials.

im-integration.description: The IM integration will be used to push direct messages to users for specific issue events. Works with the project webhook.

first-prompt.example: List all projects in my Bytebase instance
first-prompt.title: Your First Prompt
first-prompt.description: Once connected, try asking your AI assistant:

mcp.title: MCP Integration
mcp.description: Connect AI assistants to Bytebase using the Model Context Protocol.

auth.title: Authentication Required
auth.description: OAuth authentication is required on first connection. Ensure you have appropriate Bytebase permissions.

setup.add-to-config: Add this JSON to your MCP client configuration file.

quick-start.title: Option 2: CLI Commands
quick-start.description: Alternatively, use one of these CLI commands.

external-url.description: The external URL where user visits Bytebase, must start with http:// or https://.
external-url.cannot-edit-flag: External URL cannot be changed because it is set via --external-url command-line flag

ai-assistant.enabled-in-saas: Gemini AI is enabled automatically in the SaaS mode.
ai-assistant.description: Use AI to assist the database development tasks.

api-key.placeholder: An API Key usually starts with sk-*****

endpoint.description: Supply private deployment of API Endpoint.

model.description: Supply private deployment of model name.

disallow-signup.description: Users cannot self-service signup and can only be invited by the workspace admin.

password-restriction.min-length: Minimum length for password should no less than {{min}} characters
password-restriction.require-number: The password must contain at least one number
password-restriction.require-letter: The password must contain at least one letter, regardless of upper case or lower case
password-restriction.require-uppercase-letter: The password must contain at least one upper case letter
password-restriction.require-special-character: The password must contain at least one special character
password-restriction.require-reset-password-for-first-login: Require users reset their password after the 1st time login
password-restriction.password-rotation: Require users reset their password every {{day}} days

require-2fa.enable: Require two-factor authentication for all users
require-2fa.description: Users who do not have two-factor authentication enabled for their personal accounts will be forced to configure.

disallow-password-signin.require-sso-setup: A functional SSO must be created first, or users won't be able to log in.
disallow-password-signin.description: Users cannot sign in with email & password, only SSO is allowed.

access-token-duration.description: How long access tokens remain valid before requiring refresh. Shorter durations improve security.

refresh-token-duration.description: How often users must fully reauthenticate. Configuration updates require users to sign in again for changes to take effect.

inactive-session-timeout.description: The session expiration time if no activity is detected for the user. The users will be forced to log out when they reach the limit.

announcement-text.description: To hide the announcement, leave it empty.

audit-log-stdout.description: Output structured JSON audit logs to stdout for ingestion by log aggregators (Splunk, ELK, Datadog, Loki). Requires TEAM or ENTERPRISE license.

enable-debug.enable: Enable debug level log for the backend service

workspace.title: Workspace title
workspace.logo-aspect: The suggested logo size should be 5:2 aspect ratio, e.g 100 x 40.

product-improvement.description: Help improve Bytebase by sharing anonymous usage data

workspace.description: Use issue to request, review, rollout, and version database changes, suitable for teams with access control and CI & CD workflow.

sql-editor.description: Use SQL Editor to execute database changes directly, suitable for single-person use or environments that do not require supervision.

default-landing-page.default-view-changed-to-sql-editor: The workspace's default view has been changed to SQL Editor.

allow-admin-data-source.self: Allow querying the admin data source
allow-admin-data-source.description: When enabled, users can choose between admin and read-only data sources. When disabled, users are forced to use read-only data sources if configured, otherwise fall back to admin data sources.

size.description: Limit the output SQL query result size.

rows.description: Limit the output rows in SQL query result.

timeout.description: The maximum time allowed for a query to run.

watermark.description: Display watermarks on pages, including username, ID and email.

maximum-role-expiration.description: Maximum role expiration is the maximum period a role can be granted to a user, will apply on all project roles expect the project owner.

domain-restriction.description: Specify the domain that could be used as the email domain for workspace members.

members-restriction.description: Restrict sign-in to users with email addresses from the specified domains.

classification.description: Define classification levels to apply consistent masking policies across columns and databases.

setting.description: Defining custom roles. You may apply them to project members and used in custom approval.

rollout.auto-info: If all check pass, the change will be rolled out and executed automatically.
rollout.name: Rollout Policy
rollout.tip: The policy is applied to issues retroactively.
rollout.info: Tasks can be executed by users with '{{permission}}' permission or the following roles.

environment-tier.name: Environment Tier
environment-tier.description: The environment will appear differently from other environments.

environment.delete-description: Delete environment will also remove it from related instances and databases. You cannot undo this action

rule.first-match-wins: Rules are evaluated in order. First matching rule applies.

approval-flow.fallback-rules-hint: Fallback rules are evaluated only when no other rules match.

node.description: Only one approval is required on this node when there are multiple approvers.

preset-descriptions.drop-or-truncate: Require approval for DROP TABLE or TRUNCATE statements.
preset-descriptions.fallback: A catch-all rule that matches all requests. Place at the end of your rules list.
preset-descriptions.high-affected-rows: Require approval when affected rows exceed 100.

risk.description: Risk assessment automatically evaluates database changes and assigns risk levels based on the type of operation being performed.
risk.how-it-works-description: When you create a database change, Bytebase automatically analyzes the SQL statements and assigns a risk level (Low, Moderate, or High) based on predefined rules.
risk.risk-level-high: High risk operations include destructive changes like DROP TABLE, TRUNCATE, or large-scale data modifications.
risk.risk-level-moderate: Moderate risk operations include standard DDL changes like adding columns or indexes.
risk.risk-level-low: Low risk operations include SELECT queries and minor schema changes that are unlikely to cause issues.
risk.integration-description: Risk levels integrate with the Custom Approval system. You can configure approval flows that require different levels of review based on the assessed risk level of a change.

identity-provider.claims-description: The following claims were returned by the identity provider. This information can help you configure field mappings.
identity-provider.no-claims: No claims returned by the identity provider.
identity-provider.delete-warning: Are you sure you want to delete this identity provider? Users who signed up via this SSO will not be able to sign in with it anymore. If password sign-in is disabled in your workspace settings, these users may be locked out completely.

instance.archived-instances-will-not-be-displayed: Archived instances will not be displayed.
instance.force-archive-description: Force to archive. All databases will be unassigned.

sync.sync-all-tip: Database sync is asynchronous and might take a few seconds to minutes

host.title: Host
host.description: Filter by instance host. Please input the value then press the Enter.

port.title: Port
port.description: Filter by instance port. Please input the value then press the Enter.

archive.description: This will archive the selected projects. You can restore them later if needed.

delete.description: This will permanently delete the selected projects and move all databases to the default project.
delete.warning: This action cannot be undone. All project data including issues, configurations, and history will be permanently removed.

resource.delete-warning-with-resources: The "{{name}}" is used by following resources:
resource.delete-warning-retry: Please remove it from these resources then retry again.
resource.delete-warning: The "{{name}}" will be deleted. This action cannot be undone.
